task_completion.description: Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis application is open
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis window (Analysis.exe) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The FoodHistory dataset is active and loaded
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'FoodHistory' or '359 records' in the data workspace area, record count indicator, or status bar of the Epi Info interface.

task_completion.description: The frequency analysis command is configured for the ILLDUM variable
task_completion.visual_evidence: Either the 'Frequencies' dialog box is open with 'ILLDUM' selected in the variable dropdown, OR the command 'FREQ ILLDUM' (or similar) is visible in the program editor/command log.

task_completion.description: A frequency table is generated in the output window
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main output window (usually an embedded HTML viewer in Epi Info) displays a generated data table with statistical results.

task_completion.description: The output table specifically shows counts and percentages for the ILLDUM variable
task_completion.visual_evidence: The frequency table in the output window clearly indicates 'ILLDUM' as the analyzed variable and includes columns for 'Frequency' (counts) and 'Percent'.

integrity.description: Results are displayed within the genuine Epi Info 7 interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The frequency table must be rendered inside the Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis output pane, not in a standalone web browser, text editor, or spreadsheet application.

integrity.description: No external programming languages or scripts were used to bypass the software
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows, Python scripts, R scripts, or Jupyter notebooks visible calculating the frequencies.

integrity.description: The results were not manually typed
integrity.visual_evidence: The output must appear as a standard Epi Info generated table, not text manually typed into Notepad, Word, or a similar text editing program.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Software: Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis (Analysis.exe). Dataset: FoodHistory table from the EColi project, containing exactly 359 records. Target Variable: ILLDUM (illness indicator). The final output must be a frequency table generated natively within Epi Info 7, which typically displays results in an embedded HTML output window showing 'Frequency' and 'Percent' columns for the ILLDUM variable.
